'''Craig Gower''' (born 29 April 1978) is an Italian-Australian former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He is a [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual-code rugby international]], having played [[rugby league]] for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and [[rugby union]] for [[Italy national rugby union team|Italy]]. A [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] and Australian Kangaroos representative {{rlp|hb}} or {{rlp|hk}}, he played in the [[National Rugby League]] for Sydney club the [[Penrith Panthers]] (whom he captained to victory in the [[2003 NRL Premiership]]). Gower then switched rugby union, playing for French [[Top 14]] side [[Aviron Bayonnais|Bayonne]], and through grandparentage represented Italy. He returned to rugby league with the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] and then finished his playing career with one more [[National Rugby League]] season at the [[Newcastle Knights]].

==Early life==
Gower was born in [[Penrith, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of Italian descent.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/8056181.stm|title=Rugby Union | Australian Gower lands Italy call|work=BBC News|date=18 May 2009|access-date=14 February 2010}}</ref>

Gower was educated at [[Penola Catholic College, Emu Plains| MacCarthy Catholic College, Emu Plains]]. He played his junior football for the [[Colyton Colts]]  before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]].

==Rugby league career==
In Round 11 of the [[1996 ARL season]] Gower made his [[Australian Rugby League|ARL]] début for the Panthers against the [[Gold Coast Chargers]]. Gower made his representative début in his second season in first-grade when still aged only eighteen. He was selected in the Australian [[Super League (Australia)|Super League]] side for the [[ANZAC Test]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in 1997. He appeared in both Super League Tests against New Zealand and made the end of year Super League tour to Great Britain, playing in all [[Super League Test series|three Tests]] in England at halfback. Following the re-unification of Australian rugby league, Gower debuted for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[1999 State of Origin series]]. He was selected for the [[ANZAC Test]] that year but was dismissed following a disciplinary incident. However he was selected for the [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian team]] to compete in the end of season [[1999 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] he played at {{rlp|hk}} in the Kangaroos' 22–20 victory.

In 2000, Gower won the [[Hooker (rugby league)|Hooker]] of the Year award at the 2000 [[Dally M Awards]]. In 2002, Gower debuted for the [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]] team in the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] clash. Between 2002 and 2005, Gower captained the Panthers, the highlight of which was the [[2003 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Sydney Roosters]]. After that Gower was selected to go on the [[2003 Kangaroo tour]]. As 2003 NRL premiers, the Panthers travelled to England to face [[Super League VIII]] champions, the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Gower captained the Panthers at half back in their 22–4 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Craig-Gower_113/Matches/|title=Craig Gower (Old) player results, scores, news and statistics|access-date=5 June 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130919021913/http://rleague.com/players/Craig-Gower_113/Matches/|archive-date=19 September 2013}}</ref> Gower was named Australia's captain for the [[test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against France on the [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2005 Tri-Nations]] tour.<ref>[https://secure.ausport.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/98638/Australian_Rugby_League_2005.pdf Annual Report 2005] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090705170103/https://secure.ausport.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/98638/Australian_Rugby_League_2005.pdf|date=5 July 2009}} – [[Australian Rugby League]]</ref> Gower became the second Penrith Panthers player to captain his country, after [[Brad Fittler]] in 1995.

In 2006, Gower married Penrith Panthers cheerleader Amanda Flynn.{{cn|date=November 2024}} In 2007, Gower's last year at the Panthers, he ended the season with 238 career matches for the Panthers, four games short of the club record of 242 held by [[Steve Carter (rugby league)|Steve Carter]].

==Rugby union career==
On 26 June 2007, Gower switched codes by signing with French [[Top 14]] [[rugby union]] side [[Aviron Bayonnais|Bayonne]],<ref name="Aussie Gower set to switch codes">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6240798.stm|title=Aussie Gower set to switch codes|publisher=BBC|date=26 June 2007|access-date=26 June 2007}}</ref> having two years still to run on his contract at the Panthers.<ref name="French rugby my biggest challenge: Gower">{{cite web|url=http://svc002.wic110cx.server-web.com/News/Latest/NewsArticle/tabid/76/NewsId/5849/Default.aspx|title=French rugby my biggest challenge: Gower|publisher=NRL|date=26 June 2007|access-date=26 June 2007}}{{dead link|date=August 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref>

In 2008, Gower expressed his desire to play [[rugby union]] for [[Italy national rugby union team|Italy]]. He qualified for the 'Azzurri' due to his Italian grandfather. On 13 June 2009, Gower made his first appearance for [[Italy national rugby union team|Italy]] at [[fly-half]] against [[Australia national rugby union team|Australia]] after being selected for the mid-season tour of Australia and New Zealand. Italy coach [[Nick Mallett]] had initial reservations about selecting Gower.

==Return to rugby league==
Following the expiry of his union contract, Gower returned to [[rugby league]],<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/craig-gower-to-play-league-in-uk-20110810-1ilm0.html|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=10 August 2011| title=Craig Gower to play league in UK}}</ref> signing a 2-year contract with the [[London Broncos]] of the [[Super League]] starting in 2012.<ref>[http://www.league.quins.co.uk/news/13776.php Profile]{{dead link|date=August 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}, league.quins.co.uk; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref>

As a result, he did not feature in Italy's [[2011 Rugby World Cup]] campaign, and was instead selected in the [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] [[rugby league]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup qualifying]] tournament, although he missed all three matches due to injury. On 14 November 2012, Gower extended his Broncos contract by a year, making him a Bronco until the end of 2014.<ref>[http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/4678.php#.Ua7ClUBHIrU Gower extends his Broncos contract] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131227131531/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/4678.php#.Ua7ClUBHIrU |date=27 December 2013 }}, londonbroncosrl.com; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref> He was then named captain,<ref>[http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/4760.php#.Ua7CkkBHIrU Gower named captain of Broncos] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131227162758/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/4760.php#.Ua7CkkBHIrU |date=27 December 2013 }}, londonbroncosrl.com; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref> although he stood down as captain in May.<ref>[http://rleague.com/news/Gower-stands-down-as-London-skipper_119684 Gower stands down as London skipper] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130919001008/http://rleague.com/news/Gower-stands-down-as-London-skipper_119684/ |date=19 September 2013}}, rleague.com; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref>

In June 2013, Gower gained a release from his Broncos contract to return to Australia and negotiate a contract with [[National Rugby League]] club the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>[http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/4986.php#.UbHbwPlHIrU Gower joins Newcastle Knights] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514064744/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/4986.php#.UbHbwPlHIrU |date=14 May 2014 }}, londonbroncosrl.com; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref> On 8 June 2013, Gower signed a contract with the Knights for the remainder of the [[2013 NRL season]].<ref>[http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Craig-Gower-joins-the-Knights/76647 Craig Gower joins the Knights] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131005001008/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Craig-Gower-joins-the-Knights/76647 |date=5 October 2013}}, newcastleknights.com.au; accessed 27 May 2015.</ref>

Gower was named in the Italy squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130927082325/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc|url-status=usurped|archive-date=27 September 2013|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013|publisher=Rlwc2013.com|access-date=2016-03-27}}</ref> However he did not play due to the injury he suffered at the end of the [[2013 NRL season]].

At the end of the [[2013 NRL season]], Gower announced his retirement due to injury.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/craig-gower-and-bryce-gibbs-expected-to-hang-up-the-boots-20131024-2w3y6.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Craig Gower and Bryce Gibbs expected to hang up the boots|date=24 October 2014|access-date=27 May 2015}}</ref>

On 25 June 2016, Gower was inducted into the Penrith Panthers hall of fame alongside [[Grahame Moran]], [[Royce Simmons]], and [[Greg Alexander]].

==Controversies==
Gower has been involved in a number of alcohol-related off-field incidents. In 1999, Gower exposed himself to a female Irish tourist in a [[Coogee, New South Wales|Coogee]] bar, blaming his behaviour on alcohol intoxication. He was dumped from the squad and fined A$2,500 by the [[NRL]] and a further A$500 in court after pleading guilty to indecent exposure.<ref>[http://www.abc.net.au/7.30/stories/s22608.htm "Gower incident gives League image another beating"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303233219/http://www.abc.net.au/7.30/stories/s22608.htm |date=3 March 2016 }}, abc.net.au, 21 April 1999.</ref>

In December 2005, Gower was fired as Panthers captain<ref>{{cite news|title=Gower demoted after drunken binge|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4579992.stm|work=BBC News|date=4 January 2006|access-date=10 August 2007}}</ref> after incidents at a charity golf event where he argued with several guests, [[groping|groped]] the then teenage daughter of former league player [[Wayne Pearce]], chased [[Mitchell Pearce]] with a bottle before vomiting on him, streaked nude around the resort, stole and crashed a golf cart, held a butter knife<ref name="test">{{cite news|last=Gallagher|first=Brendan|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyunion/international/italy/7223016/Six-Nations-2010-Craig-Gower-making-the-most-of-fresh-start-with-Italy.html|title=Six Nations 2010: Craig Gower making the most of fresh start with Italy|newspaper=Telegraph|date=12 February 2010|access-date=14 February 2010|location=London, UK}}</ref> to the throat of a Sydney radio personality before throwing it at resort guests, and engaged in a brawl with resort security before being ejected from the official function and detained by police.<ref>{{cite news|title=NRL wants probe into Gower grope claims|url=http://www.theage.com.au/news/league/nrl-wants-probe-into-gower-grope-claims/2005/12/22/1135032121063.html|work=[[The Age]]|date=22 December 2005|access-date=10 August 2007|location=Melbourne}}</ref> He was handed a "final warning" by the [[National Rugby League]] and fined A$100,000, with A$90,000 to be paid to an NRL programme encouraging the responsible use of alcohol by league players and $10,000 to replace the destroyed golf cart.<ref>{{cite news|title=NRL gives Gower final warning and A$100,000 fine|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/section/4/story.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10364852|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|date=23 January 2006|access-date=10 August 2007}}</ref> Gower was "deeply unhappy" that the [[Penrith Panthers]] club did not defend his reputation, and at one stage threatened to "walk" from the club.<ref>{{cite news|first=Greg|last=Prichard|title=Gower's ultimatum: defend me or I'll walk|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/gowers-ultimatum-defend-me-or-ill-walk/2006/02/05/1139074110257.html|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=6 February 2006|access-date=10 August 2007}}</ref>

Allegedly inebriated with alcohol in a bar at [[Kings Cross, New South Wales|Kings Cross]] on 11 February 2007, Gower allegedly tried to kiss one man before biting him on the neck and sparking a brawl, and is accused of assaulting another man.<ref>{{cite news|first=Jamie|last=Pandaram|title=Police set to charge Gower|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/police-set-to-charge-gower/2007/03/22/1174153255058.html|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=23 March 2007|access-date=10 August 2007|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927012320/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/police-set-to-charge-gower/2007/03/22/1174153255058.html|archive-date=27 September 2007}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|first=Roy|last=Masters|author-link=Roy Masters (sport)|title=Time for Craig to face demons|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/time-for-craig-to-face-demons/2007/02/12/1171128898565.html|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=13 February 2007|access-date=10 August 2007}}</ref> The Panthers club controversially reappointed Gower as captain in 2007, claiming the Peppermint Lounge incident was just a media "beat-up".<ref>{{cite news|title=Enough is enough|url=http://origin.www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,21237481-5001023,00.html|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|date=17 February 2007|access-date=10 August 2007|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080924063048/http://origin.www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,21237481-5001023,00.html|archive-date=24 September 2008|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Australian swimmer [[Dawn Fraser]] said Gower was unfit to be captain, due to his alleged lewd behaviour at the charity golf event which she attended,<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,,21006250-23214,00.html|title=Gower 'shouldn't be captain'|work=Fox Sports|date=3 January 2007|last=Clark|first=Laine}}</ref> and [[Sarah Maddison]], spokesperson for the Women's Electoral Lobby, said "reappointing Craig Gower would send all the wrong messages."<ref>{{cite news|first=James|last=Phelps|title=Leave Craig alone|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,21011973-5001023,00.html|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|date=5 January 2007|access-date=10 August 2007}}</ref>
